title: linaria multicaulis subsp. galioides
intro: Linaria multicaulis subsp. galioides, also known as toadflax or butter and eggs, is a plant species in the family Plantaginaceae. It is a perennial herb with a history of use in traditional medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, particularly as an expectorant and anti-inflammatory agent. The plant has been reported to possess various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Linaria multicaulis subsp. galioides is commonly prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and may be used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for colds, coughs, and other respiratory conditions.
